text.title: AI Accessibility Automation Capabilities 2026
text.summary: AI vs traditional tool comparison, enterprise platform survey, and new 2026 efficiency features including ADA Title II compliance context.

list.item: Issue detection: traditional 30–50% coverage; AI-enhanced 60–70% with context
Code remediation: traditional manual; AI suggests fixes with code snippets
VPAT creation: traditional hours; AI generates draft in seconds
Issue prioritization: traditional manual triage; AI ranks by user impact and traffic
Alt text generation: traditional manual writing; AI suggests from image content
Deque axe DevTools Pro — AI issue extraction, fix suggestions, chatbot; ADA Title II reporting
Level Access — Ask Level AI chatbot; no programmatic scan API
Evinced — AI continuous testing; REST API for CI/CD
AudioEye — AI remediation with human oversight (not overlay-based)
3Play Media — ADA-compliant captions (AI + human review, 99% accuracy); REST API
Rev.ai — 80–90% AI caption accuracy; requires human review step

rule.statement: For state/local government or higher-education digital products, document WCAG 2.2 AA conformance with a VPAT 2.5 — AI drafting compresses this from hours to seconds but requires human legal review before external use.
rule.rationale: The ADA Title II final rule (effective April 2026) requires WCAG 2.2 AA compliance for state/local government digital products. Late adopters face immediate legal risk. VPAT drafts are legally significant documents.

list.item: 3Play Media — AI transcription + human review (99% accuracy); REST API
Rev.ai — automated captions 80–90% accuracy; needs human review
YouTube Auto-Captions — free but always needs manual review
AI audio descriptions: YouDescribe (crowdsourced + AI), AI.Film (automated scenes), Descriptive Video Works (AI draft + human polish)
Live captioning: Google Live Captions, Microsoft Teams, Zoom (with speaker ID)
